news.title: In a first, Ranchi civic body collects ₹108 crore in taxes
news.para: Ranchi Municipal Corporation has achieved a record Rs 108 crore in tax collection this financial year, with property tax contributing a significant Rs 100 crore. This surge, attributed to intensified drives and digital payment systems, will fund civic infrastructure improvements. Traders note the convenience of online payment systems has boosted compliance.

news.title: Belagavi civic body revises property tax rates starting April 1
news.para: Belagavi City Corporation has revised property tax rates, increasing them by 3% for residential properties and 4% for commercial, industrial, and plots, effective April 1. This move aims to boost annual revenue by Rs 2-3 crore, with a 5% discount offered for early payment.

news.title: Download allotment letters from Lucknow development body's website
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority now requires allottees to download allotment letters from its website within three months to streamline the process and enhance transparency. Failure to do so will result in automatic cancellation without notice, with registration amounts refunded after deductions. This move aims to reduce delays and prevent fraudulent practices.

news.title: Ahmedabad development body's new draft development plan for 2041 likely in June
news.para: The Ahmedabad Urban Development Authority's (Auda) long-awaited city development plan is set for public release in June, focusing on a 20-year vision up to 2041 and 2047. This ambitious plan aims to significantly upgrade city infrastructure, incorporating new areas and potentially relocating industries to reduce air pollution ahead of the 2030 Commonwealth Games and a bid for the 2036 Olympics.

news.title: Delhi: NDMC tax reform may cut property tax up to 50%
news.para: The New Delhi Municipal Council plans to implement a Unit Area Method for property tax. This new system could slash taxes by 30 to 50 percent, especially for older, self-occupied properties. It aims for fairer taxation by assessing properties based on unit area rather than rental value. Traders welcome the move but seek clarity on tax rates and enforcement.

news.title: Fire safety must stay in building code: BIS panel
news.para: A technical committee of the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) has opposed the deregulation cell's proposal to remove fire safety provisions from the National Building Code, citing recent fatal fires. While accepting some recommendations, the committee insists fire safety must remain in the code, not as a separate handbook, emphasizing its crucial role in preventing death traps.

news.title: CAG questions land allotment by Telangana government to Amazon in Pharma City
news.para: The CAG has raised concerns over land allotment for a data centre within the Hyderabad Pharma City. This move deviates from the original plan for a pharmaceutical hub. The audit highlights potential job losses and delays in project implementation. The government maintains the project is on track. Weak monitoring and land management issues were also flagged.

news.title: AAI says give clearances for building heights on a case-to-case basis in Mumbai
news.para: A new AAI circular offers hope for stalled Juhu redevelopment projects by establishing a committee to assess building height restrictions near HF transmitters. This move aims to allow case-by-case evaluations, potentially easing current limitations and enabling viable vertical growth while ensuring aviation safety.

news.title: Thiruvananthapuram: Neopolis IT project faces delay due to centre's expansion plan
news.para: The Neopolis IT township project in Thiruvananthapuram may face delays. The Union government plans to expand the scheme to around 100 cities across India. This move could lead to a fresh evaluation of the Thiruvananthapuram project. Officials are reworking proposals, including for Kannur. The project aims to create a futuristic IT hub with residential and commercial facilities.

news.title: New Town Kolkata development body issues notices to over 65 buildings
news.para: New Town Kolkata Development Authority (NKDA) has issued stop-operation notices to 69 buildings for commercial use violations. Following initial identification of 1,200 buildings, inspections confirmed gross anomalies in 69, with a block-wise assessment of nearly 1,000 suspected properties ongoing. NKDA has also revised its NOC process for commercial use in residential buildings.

news.title: Kirloskar Industries' realty arm raises ₹1,150 crore term loan from ICICI Bank
news.para: Avante Spaces, Kirloskar Industries' real estate arm, secured a Rs 1,150 crore term loan from ICICI Bank for a Grade A commercial development in Pune's Kothrud. This financing, structured over 44 months at 8% interest, underscores lender confidence in established urban locations and well-backed projects.

news.title: Vedanta moves SC against Adani Group's ₹14,535-crore bid for Jaiprakash Associates
news.para: Vedanta has taken its battle to the Supreme Court, aiming to stop the Adani Group from clinching a deal to buy Jaiprakash Associates Ltd. Previously, the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal shot down requests to pause this controversial transaction. The Adani Group has responded swiftly with a caveat, seeking to protect its interests.

news.title: Chhattisgarh leads in rural housing, completes over six lakh homes in FY26
news.para: Chhattisgarh has achieved a significant milestone by completing over six lakh rural houses in the financial year 2025-26. This marks the highest number of houses completed in a single financial year in the state. The achievement reflects a coordinated effort across central and state housing schemes. The government is committed to 'housing for all', ensuring eligible families receive support.

news.title: DLF concludes deal to sell IT-SEZ, vacant land in Kolkata for ₹710 crore
news.para: DLF has completed the sale of its Kolkata IT SEZ project and vacant land. The deal, valued at Rs 710 crore, was finalised with Srijan Group. This move is part of DLF's strategy to monetise its commercial assets. The transactions involved the transfer of DLF TechPark II and nearly 18 acres of land.

news.title: ED attaches over ₹270-crore assets of former Lodha Developers' director Rajendra Lodha
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has confiscated properties worth over 270 crore rupees in connection to a high-profile money laundering case. Ex-Lodha Developers director, Rajendra Lodha, who was taken into custody earlier this year, stands accused of manipulating company resources through dubious land deals and unauthorized asset sales.

news.title: Lodha Developers raises ₹500 crore through issue of debentures
news.para: Lodha Developers has successfully raised Rs 500 crore through the issuance of non-convertible debentures. The company's board approved the allotment of 50,000 debentures at Rs 1 lakh each. This private placement comes with an interest rate of 8.52 per cent per annum. Lodha Developers is a prominent real estate firm with a significant presence in key Indian cities.

news.title: NCLAT modifies insolvency order against Vatika, confines it to Gurugram-based Project Aspirations
news.para: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal has limited insolvency proceedings against realty firm Vatika. The proceedings will now focus solely on the Project Aspirations in Gurgaon. This decision modifies a previous National Company Law Tribunal order. The appellate tribunal confirmed that debt and default were proven. The insolvency resolution process cannot encompass all of Vatika's other projects.

news.title: Over 62,500 mortgages approved in UK in February 2026: Bank of England
news.para: British lenders approved the most mortgages in three months in February, while consumer credit grew at its fastest pace in nearly two years.  However, recent signs indicate caution in the housing market, with higher borrowing costs expected to push up mortgage rates following the Iran war outbreak.

news.title: Pune: Talathi & circle officer blocked mutation plea, lapses followed
news.para: A report by the Vikas Kharge-led panel revealed that revenue officials initially flagged irregularities in the Mundhwa land sale, blocking mutation. However, procedural lapses and failure to inform higher authorities weakened scrutiny. The tehsildar's neglect of duty and improper actions regarding the Botanical Survey of India's lease complicated the matter.

news.title: Lok Sabha passes insolvency law amendments; FM says bill will help maximise value for stakeholders
news.para: The Lok Sabha has approved a bill to update the insolvency law. This amendment introduces strict timelines for resolving stressed companies. It also brings in an option for out-of-court settlements. The framework will now support cross-border insolvency processes. These changes aim to improve the efficiency of the insolvency process and boost investor confidence.
